title: Paperclip Desktop
description: A guided tour of the Paperclip Desktop Electron app - from the main process and server lifecycle to the build pipeline and release automation.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of Node.js and TypeScript

steps.title: Project Overview
steps.description: Paperclip Desktop is an Electron wrapper around the Paperclip AI orchestration server. It bundles the server, a dedicated runtime, and the Paperclip UI into a native desktop app with auto-updates, code signing, and a splash screen. The entire Electron shell is only about 2000 lines of code.

steps.title: Main Process - The Heart
steps.description: The highlighted file is the core of the app. It manages the entire server lifecycle - port discovery, process spawning, splash screen progress, health checks, and UI loading. It also handles orphan cleanup and graceful shutdown.

steps.title: IPC & Preload Security
steps.description: Electron's security model is enforced through preload scripts with context isolation. The main preload bridges the renderer to the auto-updater. The splash preload feeds real-time progress events to the splash screen. Node integration is disabled - all communication flows through these narrow IPC channels.

steps.title: Auto-Update System
steps.description: The highlighted file checks GitHub Releases every 4 hours for new versions. Updates download in the background, then prompt the user to restart. The renderer receives live status updates through the preload IPC bridge.

steps.title: Build Pipeline Scripts
steps.description: This directory contains the multi-stage build pipeline. The core scripts bundle the server, build the upstream UI, launch dev mode, and orchestrate staged macOS releases. A second layer handles code signing and Apple notarization.

steps.title: Packaging & CI/CD
steps.description: The packaging configuration targets DMG, NSIS, and AppImage across platforms. The build resources hold macOS entitlements and app icons. GitHub Actions handle a two-phase release: build and publish first, then Apple notarization as a separate approval step.

steps.title: Architecture Documentation
steps.description: This directory contains detailed technical documentation. The highlighted files cover the full architecture reference, a step-by-step release checklist, and the staged macOS packaging flow with signing and notarization details.

steps.title: The Full Picture
steps.description: That's the complete Paperclip Desktop architecture. For deeper dives, start with the architecture reference in the documentation directory or explore the code directly.
